items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan presented to council
items.summary: Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, indicating upcoming changes to how the city's library system operates and serves residents.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus floor space for upgrades
items.summary: A historic building at 405 Kipling Street is being elevated from a lower to higher historic status, which allows the owner to add 5,000 square feet of floor area in exchange for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This incentivizes preservation of older downtown buildings.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop two properties on San Antonio Road with an eight-story building containing 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This significant project will reshape the area and is undergoing City Council review.

items.headline: California grant awarded to Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José secured funding from the California Natural Resources Agency for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This investment supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.

items.headline: City approves pavement replacement contract for 2026 local streets project
items.summary: Council awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, addressing deteriorating street conditions across San José neighborhoods.

items.headline: Storm drain fees increase 3% for residential customers
items.summary: Monthly storm and surface water drainage charges will rise to $18.14 per residential unit in fiscal year 2027, a 3% increase tied to inflation.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan being updated with new concepts
items.summary: The City Council is reviewing draft land use and transportation concepts for the San Antonio Road corridor on April 6, signaling potential changes to zoning, development patterns, or transit in this important area.
